items.question: Is BeAFox really free?
items.answer: Yes — getting started is completely free. You can complete lessons, get to know Bea, and walk through your first situation. BeAFox Unlimited unlocks full access.

items.question: What makes Bea different from other finance apps?
items.answer: Bea isn’t a chatbot quoting Wikipedia. Bea understands your situation—whether you’re an apprentice, student, or early-career professional—and recommends the steps that matter for you now. Personalized, neutral, and no product sales.

items.question: Is my data safe?
items.answer: Absolutely. BeAFox is GDPR compliant, hosted in Germany, and doesn’t sell user data. We’re independent—no bank, no insurer, no product pushing.

items.question: Who is BeAFox for?
items.answer: Anyone aged 16–30 who wants to take charge of their money—especially apprentices, students, and career starters. Schools and training companies also use BeAFox for their audiences.

experienceSection.title: We speak from
experienceSection.quote: "First paycheck, spent it all, month over – and then came the bills, late notices, and debt. Nobody ever taught us how money really works."
experienceSection.paragraph1: We were apprentices ourselves – young, excited, finally earning our own money. But without knowing anything about budgets, saving, or contracts, we burned through every cent. Overdrafts, installment traps, subscription pitfalls. What started as freedom quickly turned into real stress: sleepless nights, shame, and the feeling of being completely alone with it.
experienceSection.paragraph2: That exact feeling was the spark behind BeAFox. We don't want the next generation to repeat these mistakes. So we built an app that teaches financial knowledge the way we wished someone had taught us back then: honestly, clearly, and without judgment.
experienceSection.paragraph3: If even one person makes one fewer mistake because of BeAFox – it was all worth it.

items.question: Are there rewards or progress indicators?
items.answer: Yes! You can earn points, complete missions and track your progress at any time – learning made fun. The gamified system motivates you to learn continuously and improve your financial literacy.

items.question: How much does BeAFox cost for educational institutions?
items.answer: To make financial education accessible to all students, we offer fair, tiered pricing for schools. For schools the price is €1 per student per year. For businesses we offer tiered pricing from 10 licenses from €3.99 per month. Contact us for a custom quote.

items.question: Is there a teacher or admin access?
items.answer: Yes, for teachers, trainers and admins we offer a comprehensive dashboard where you can create student accounts, manage classes and view participants' learning progress in detail. The dashboard also provides analytics and reports.

items.question: Is BeAFox really independent and neutral?
items.answer: Yes, absolutely! We focus purely on knowledge transfer. No financial products are promoted in the app, so the content feels honest and trustworthy. Our mission is to provide real financial education, not to sell products.

items.question: How does the gamified system work?
items.answer: With a gamified system of points, missions, rewards and leaderboards, learning no longer feels like a chore. Students stay motivated and learn sustainably. You can level up, unlock achievements and compete with others.

items.question: Can I use BeAFox as an individual?
items.answer: Yes! BeAFox is also available to individuals. You get full access to all lessons and can learn at your own pace. We offer different subscription models: Monthly (€4.99), annual (€3.99/month) or lifetime (€49.99 one-time).

items.question: How evidence-based is BeAFox?
items.answer: We use evidence-based teaching methods to create lessons that successfully improve your financial knowledge. Our research project with over 500 participants has shown that our methods work. Content is developed by financial experts and educators.

items.question: What is the difference between BeAFox for Schools and BeAFox for Business?
items.answer: BeAFox for Schools targets educational institutions and offers a monitoring dashboard for teachers. BeAFox for Business targets training companies and additionally includes regular workshops (every 6 months), official certificates and extended reporting.

items.question: Can I try BeAFox before buying?
items.answer: Yes, we're happy to offer a free trial. Contact us for more information and a demo. Schools can start a pilot project to try BeAFox with no obligation.

items.question: How often is new content added?
items.answer: We continuously work on new lessons and features. All users receive regular updates with new content and improvements. Our goal is to publish new lessons monthly and larger feature updates quarterly.

items.question: Is there support if I have questions?
items.answer: Yes, our support is there for you around the clock and will answer your requests as quickly and helpfully as possible. Contact us anytime via email, the contact form or directly in the app!

items.question: Does BeAFox work on all devices?
items.answer: BeAFox is available as an app for iOS and Android. The monitoring dashboard for teachers and trainers works in the browser on all devices (desktop, tablet, smartphone). The app syncs your progress automatically across devices.

items.question: How secure is my data?
items.answer: Data protection and security are our top priorities. We use modern encryption and strictly comply with GDPR. Your data is stored securely and never shared with third parties.

items.question: Can I export my progress?
items.answer: Yes, you can export your progress and certificates at any time. For schools and businesses we also offer detailed reports and analytics as PDF or Excel export.

items.question: Are there workshops or training sessions?
items.answer: Yes! We offer interactive financial education workshops for schools, companies and associations. Workshops can be held on-site or online and are led by experienced facilitators. Contact us for more information.

items.question: What is included in the price?
items.answer: The price includes full app access, all learning modules, monitoring dashboards (for schools/businesses), support and regular updates. Business packages also include workshops and certificates.

items.question: Can I cancel anytime?
items.answer: For individuals: The monthly subscription can be cancelled at any time. Annual subscriptions run for the agreed term. For schools and businesses, individual agreements apply and can be flexible.

items.question: Are there discounts for multiple classes or locations?
items.answer: Yes, we offer tiered pricing for larger institutions. The more students or employees, the lower the price per person. Contact us for a custom offer tailored to your needs.

story.problemText: Our founders are only 21 years old and know from their own experience what it feels like to be completely at sea with money. During their training they got into debt themselves but fought their way back. That personal experience motivated them to build a solution to help other young people avoid these challenges.
story.solutionText: With BeAFox we've finally created a way to teach finance – often seen as boring – in a lasting and understandable way. We want the next generation to gain financial knowledge independently and with motivation. No pressure, no feeling of being forced. Through gamification, clear structure, and evidence-based methods we make financial education an experience that's fun.

faq.subtitle: Alles, was Sie über BeAFox für Schulen wissen müssen.

items.question: Wie lange dauert die Einrichtung?
items.answer: Die Einrichtung geht schnell und unkompliziert. Nach der Beratung richten wir die App für Ihre Schule ein und erstellen Accounts für alle Schüler. Der gesamte Prozess dauert in der Regel nur wenige Tage.

items.question: Benötigen die Schüler eigene Geräte?
items.answer: Nein, BeAFox funktioniert auf allen gängigen Geräten – Smartphones, Tablets und Computern. Die Schüler können die App auf ihren eigenen Geräten nutzen oder auf schuleigenen Geräten.

items.question: Wie werden die Daten geschützt?
items.answer: Alle Daten werden sicher und DSGVO-konform gespeichert. Wir legen großen Wert auf Datenschutz und Sicherheit. Ihre Schülerdaten sind bei uns in besten Händen.

items.question: Können wir BeAFox testen, bevor wir uns entscheiden?
items.answer: Ja, gerne! Wir bieten eine kostenlose Beratung und können Ihnen auch eine Testphase ermöglichen. Kontaktieren Sie uns einfach für weitere Informationen.

items.question: Was passiert, wenn wir BeAFox nicht mehr nutzen möchten?
items.answer: Kein Problem! Sie können BeAFox jederzeit monatlich kündigen. Es gibt keine langfristigen Verträge oder versteckten Kosten.

items.question: Gibt es Schulungen für Lehrkräfte?
items.answer: Ja, wir bieten Fortbildungen für Lehrkräfte an, in denen wir das System, das Dashboard und die besten Praktiken für den Einsatz im Unterricht erklären.

content.text: These General Terms and Conditions (Terms) apply to all contracts between BeAFox UG (haftungsbeschränkt), Siemensweg 2, 93073 Neutraubling, HR Regensburg, Registration Number HRB 21689 (hereinafter "BeAFox") and its customers regarding the use of the BeAFox App and associated services. BeAFox is a company operating in the field of financial education targeting private consumers (age group 16-30 years), educational institutions, and businesses.

content.subtitle: Contact and Data Protection
content.text: Information regarding data protection, including the processing of personal data under the GDPR and the Telecommunications Digital Services Data Protection Act (TDDDG), can be found in our Privacy Policy at https://beafox.app/datenschutz. Further information about BeAFox can be found at https://beafox.app.

BeAFox offers a mobile learning app for financial education. The app enables users to acquire and deepen financial knowledge in a gamified manner. The app is supported by "Bea", an AI-powered assistant (see Section 17 on Artificial Intelligence).

content.subtitle: Services
content.text: For private consumers, individualized subscriptions are offered with access to lessons, practice exercises, and premium features. For educational institutions and businesses, additional services such as workshops, monitoring dashboards, teaching materials, and certificates are offered.

content.subtitle: Scope of Services
content.text: The exact scope of services is determined by the respective contract offer and the description displayed in the checkout. Additional services are agreed upon separately.

content.subtitle: Contract Forms as Offers
content.text: The product descriptions and prices displayed on the website www.beafox.app are invitations to make offers (§ 145 German Civil Code), not binding offers.

content.subtitle: Contract Conclusion via Website Checkout (Stripe)
content.text: Contracts for subscriptions are concluded through: (1) selection of a plan in the checkout, (2) confirmation by "Pay Now" button (customer's offer), (3) confirmation by BeAFox after successful payment processing by Stripe. Payment processing is handled by Stripe, Inc. (www.stripe.com). BeAFox receives confirmation of successful payments but does not store payment data.

content.subtitle: Contract Conclusion via App Stores
content.text: For in-app purchases via Apple App Store or Google Play Store, the payment contract is concluded exclusively between the user and the respective app store operator. BeAFox merely provides the content and is not a party to the payment contract. The terms and conditions of the app store operator apply supplementary.

content.subtitle: Contract Conclusion by Email or in Writing
content.text: The contract can also be concluded in writing by mail or email. Acceptance of an offer is made by written confirmation from BeAFox.

content.subtitle: Contract Language
content.text: Contracts are concluded in the German language. This is also the binding contract language.

content.subtitle: Right of Withdrawal
content.text: Consumers (natural persons who do not act for professional or commercial purposes) have the right to withdraw from the contract within 14 days of contract conclusion (§ 312g German Civil Code). For contracts for the purchase of digital content, the withdrawal period is 14 days from the date of contract conclusion (§ 312g para. 2 German Civil Code).

content.subtitle: Loss of Right of Withdrawal
content.text: The right of withdrawal expires early if BeAFox has begun performing the service and the consumer (a) has explicitly agreed that BeAFox begins performance before the withdrawal period expires, and (b) has acknowledged awareness of the loss of the right of withdrawal (§ 312g para. 2 sentence 2 German Civil Code). In the event of withdrawal, only the service provided is charged.

content.subtitle: Exercise of Right of Withdrawal
content.text:
To exercise the right of withdrawal, you must inform BeAFox using an unambiguous statement (e.g., a letter by mail, fax, or email). The statement can be made using our model withdrawal form. You can also use the app settings to cancel your subscription (available in account settings).

content.subtitle: Consequences of Withdrawal
content.text: If you withdraw from this contract, we will refund all payments received from you immediately and no later than 14 days from the day we receive notification of your withdrawal (§ 312g para. 3 German Civil Code). This refund will be made using the same payment method you used for the original transaction, unless you have provided different instructions.
BeAFox undertakes to provide the agreed-upon services carefully and to the best of its knowledge and ability.

content.subtitle: Availability
content.text: BeAFox endeavors to ensure high availability of the app and aims for an average annual availability of at least 98%. Maintenance work, further development, or technical issues may temporarily restrict access. Planned maintenance windows will be announced at least 48 hours in advance, where possible, via the app or website. There is no claim for continuous uninterrupted availability at all times.

content.subtitle: Changes to Features
content.text: BeAFox reserves the right to change, expand, or remove features of the app. Essential features will not be removed without prior notice. Users will be informed of material feature changes at least two weeks in advance. There is no claim to specific features unless these are expressly defined as essential in the contract.

content.subtitle: Updates
content.text: Users are obligated to install available security and feature updates to the app within a reasonable timeframe (typically within 30 days). Older versions of the app may no longer be supported after notification. Critical security updates should be installed immediately.

content.subtitle: Prices for Consumers (B2C)
content.text:
The prices displayed on the website and in the app for private consumers are total prices including applicable value-added tax (VAT) in accordance with the German Price Indication Ordinance (PAngV § 1). The total price is the price the consumer pays.

Currently offered plans (gross prices incl. 19% VAT):
- Monthly subscription: EUR 4.99 per month
- Annual subscription: EUR 44.99 per year
- Lifetime access: EUR 49.99 (one-time)

content.subtitle: Prices for Businesses and Educational Institutions (B2B)
content.text: For businesses and educational institutions, prices are provided upon request. These may be stated as net prices plus applicable VAT. The applicable tax rate will be shown on the invoice.

content.subtitle: Website Payments (Stripe)
content.text: Payment processing for subscriptions via the website is handled by the payment service provider Stripe, Inc. Stripe processes the payment data. BeAFox only receives confirmation of successful payments and does not store payment data. Payment information can be found in the Privacy Policy.

content.subtitle: Payment Terms
content.text: Payment is due immediately (payment method: card payment via Stripe). For automatic subscriptions, the deduction occurs on the first day of the billing period (monthly or annually as chosen).

content.subtitle: Default Interest for Consumers (B2C)
content.text: For payment default by consumers, the provisions of § 288 para. 1 German Civil Code apply: default interest is 5 percentage points above the base interest rate per year. The base interest rate is set by the German Federal Bank.

content.subtitle: Default Interest for Businesses and Educational Institutions (B2B)
content.text: For payment default by businesses (including educational institutions), the provisions of § 288 para. 2 German Civil Code apply: default interest is 9 percentage points above the base interest rate per year.

content.subtitle: Terms and Automatic Renewal
content.text: Subscriptions automatically renew for the agreed-upon term (monthly, annually, or no renewal for lifetime access) unless canceled on time. The cancellation notice period is one month to the end of the term. Cancellation can be made in the app settings.

content.subtitle: Price Changes
content.text: BeAFox reserves the right to change prices. Changes will be communicated at least four weeks before taking effect for new contracts. For existing subscriptions, price changes apply from the next renewal. Consumers can cancel the contract if they do not agree with a price change.

The customer is granted a simple, non-transferable, and non-exclusive right to use the app and its content for the purpose agreed upon in this contract. Sharing access credentials with third parties is not permitted. Access is strictly personal and may not be shared or transferred.

content.subtitle: Restrictions on Use
content.text: The app may only be used for the agreed-upon private or business purpose. Commercial use of content, copying, reproducing, or distributing content without written consent from BeAFox is not permitted. Reverse engineering, decompiling, or disassembling the app is prohibited.

content.subtitle: Prohibited Actions
content.text: It is particularly prohibited to: use screenshots or content for commercial purposes; copy, reverse engineer, or manipulate the app; use automated systems (bots, scrapers) to use the app; introduce malware; rent, pledge, or give away the app or parts of it; overload the system or conduct denial-of-service attacks.

content.subtitle: Right to Object
content.text: Violations of these provisions may lead to immediate account suspension. BeAFox reserves the right to take legal action, including pursuing damage claims.

The processing of personal data is carried out in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Telecommunications Digital Services Data Protection Act (TDDDG). BeAFox takes data protection seriously and maintains a data protection management system.

content.subtitle: Privacy Policy
content.text: The complete Privacy Policy with information about data collection, processing, storage, your rights, and objection options can be found at: https://beafox.app/datenschutz

content.subtitle: Data Processing Agreement for Schools and Businesses
content.text: For educational institutions and businesses, a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) in accordance with Article 28 GDPR may be required, particularly when processing student or employee data. A DPA will be concluded if needed before contract conclusion.

content.subtitle: Data Deletion and Retention
content.text: After termination of the contractual relationship or upon request, personal data will be deleted unless legal retention obligations prevent this (e.g., accounting record retention requirements under § 257 German Commercial Code). The retention period will be specified in the Privacy Policy.

content.subtitle: Warranty Obligations
content.text: BeAFox is obligated to provide digital content and services in accordance with § 327f et seq. German Civil Code of a certain quality and functionality and to maintain this quality throughout the agreed contract period.

content.subtitle: Update Obligation
content.text: BeAFox is obligated to keep digital content current and to provide required, security-critical updates. Users must install available security updates and other critical updates as per these Terms.

content.subtitle: Rights for Defects
content.text: For defects in digital content (functional problems, missing promised features), statutory provisions apply. Users can initially report defective content to support for remedy. BeAFox will endeavor to remedy defects promptly. A claim for specific features or content exists only insofar as these have been expressly agreed upon.

content.subtitle: No Warranty for Completeness and Currency
content.text: BeAFox makes no warranty regarding the completeness, accuracy, or currency of the information provided in the app. The content serves general educational purposes and does not constitute individual personal financial advice (see Section 12).

content.subtitle: AI Content and Liability
content.text: Content generated by "Bea" (AI assistant) may occasionally contain errors or inaccuracies. BeAFox endeavors to correct such errors but makes no guarantee regarding the error-free nature of AI-generated content.

All content of the app (texts, illustrations, graphics, design, logic, software, videos, animations, Lottie animations) is protected by copyright and is the property of BeAFox or its licensors. The content is subject to German copyright law (UrhG) and international copyright agreements.

content.subtitle: Prohibition on Reproduction and Distribution
content.text: Reproduction, publication, distribution, or commercial use of the content is not permitted without written consent. This applies to parts of the content as well. The right to make private copies under § 53 UrhG is compatible with non-commercial use but not with further distribution.

content.subtitle: Specific Prohibitions
content.text:
It is particularly prohibited to:
- Use screenshots, graphics, or text excerpts for commercial purposes or publication
- Copy, decompile, or reverse engineer the app or parts of it
- Share access credentials or share with others
- Use automated systems (bots, scrapers) to collect or distribute content
- Manipulate or modify the app
- Re-publish content on other platforms

content.subtitle: Consequences of Violations
content.text: In the event of violations of these provisions, BeAFox reserves the right to: (1) immediately suspend access to the app, (2) take legal action, including issuing cease and desist notices and pursuing damage claims under §§ 97 et seq. German Copyright Law, (3) assert injunctive relief.

sections.title: Obligations of Educational Institutions and Businesses

content.subtitle: Scope of Use and Purpose Limitation
content.text: The app may only be used for the institution's own employees, students, trainees, or university students. Transfer to third parties or use by third parties is not permitted. The use must be for the purpose of financial education or vocational training.

content.subtitle: Minimum User Numbers and License Scope
content.text: Agreed-upon minimum user numbers or license scope must be maintained. Exceeding these requires contract adjustment and may result in additional costs. BeAFox reserves the right to monitor user numbers.

content.subtitle: Youth Protection and Consent
content.text: When used by minors, it must be ensured that use complies with legal requirements. For users under 16 years, parental consent is required (Art. 8 GDPR). The institution bears responsibility for compliance with this requirement.

content.subtitle: Responsibility for Access Credentials and Security
content.text: The institution is responsible for secure management and control of access credentials. The administrator must ensure that access credentials are not shared with unauthorized persons. In case of loss or misuse of access credentials, BeAFox must be informed immediately. The institution is liable for misuse by its users.

content.subtitle: Compliance with Terms of Use
content.text: The institution undertakes to inform all users about the Terms and conditions of use and to ensure compliance. Violations by users may lead to account suspension.

content.subtitle: Regular Review
content.text: BeAFox reserves the right to verify compliance with these provisions and to suspend access in the event of violations.

The content, calculators, and information provided by BeAFox do NOT constitute investment advice, financial advice, wealth management advice, tax advice, or legal advice. They serve exclusively for general education and knowledge transfer. The content is based on publicly available information and makes no claim to completeness or currency.

content.subtitle: User Responsibility
content.text: Decisions regarding financial investments, insurance, retirement planning, tax strategies, or other financial decisions are made by the user independently and at their own risk. BeAFox assumes no liability for economic or tax decisions made by users based on the provided content.

content.subtitle: No Personal Recommendations
content.text: The content contains no recommendations for specific financial products, securities, real estate, or investment strategies for the user's personal situation. BeAFox is independent and neutral.

content.subtitle: Disclaimer
content.text: Liability for economic consequences, tax consequences, or financial losses arising from the use of the BeAFox app is excluded. Users should consult professional advice (tax advisor, financial advisor, attorney) before making important financial decisions.

content.subtitle: Liability for Intent and Gross Negligence
content.text: BeAFox is liable without limitation for damages resulting from intent or gross negligence of BeAFox, its representatives, or agents.

content.subtitle: NO Exclusion of Liability for Personal Injury
content.text: IMPORTANT: A limitation or exclusion of liability for injury to life, body, or health is expressly NOT permitted and NOT subject to these Terms. This is prohibited by § 309 No. 7a German Civil Code for terms and conditions between businesses and consumers. Liability for damages from injury to life, body, or health remains fully in effect.

content.subtitle: Liability for Ordinary Negligence
content.text: In case of negligence (not gross negligence), BeAFox is only liable for breach of a material contractual obligation whose proper performance is necessary to enable contract performance at all (cardinal obligation). Liability is limited to damages foreseeable at contract conclusion and typical for the contract (e.g., direct financial losses from defective content).

content.subtitle: Liability Limits
content.text: Excluded from liability limits are: (1) injury to life, body, or health; (2) intent or gross negligence; (3) guarantees within the meaning of § 443 German Civil Code; (4) liability under Product Liability Act (ProdHaftG); (5) statutory liability for data protection breaches under GDPR.

content.subtitle: Liability Exclusions
content.text: To the extent permitted, BeAFox is not liable for: (a) data loss or data corruption unless caused by intent or gross negligence; (b) economic decisions or financial losses that users make based on app content; (c) business interruption or lost profits; (d) indirect or consequential damages; (e) damages from misuse or deviation from this contract; (f) actions by third parties, in particular app store operators, payment service providers, or other service providers, insofar as BeAFox is not at fault.

content.subtitle: Product Liability
content.text: Liability under Product Liability Act (ProdHaftG) remains unaffected by liability limitations. BeAFox is liable without limitation for damages resulting from defective products (§ 1 ProdHaftG).

content.subtitle: Application of Liability Limitations
content.text: The foregoing liability limitations do NOT apply to injury to life, body, or health caused by BeAFox or its representatives.

content.subtitle: Cancellation by Consumers
content.text: Consumers can cancel subscriptions at any time with one month's notice to the end of the billing period (month or year). Cancellation can be made through: (1) cancellation button in the app (account settings); (2) in writing by mail or email to info@beafox.app; (3) by phone upon request. Cancellation must be confirmed in writing.

content.subtitle: Cancellation Button pursuant to § 312k German Civil Code
content.text: Consumers can cancel their subscription via a cancellation button in the app settings. This is a simple and free procedure without additional obstacles (§ 312k para. 2 German Civil Code). The cancellation button is clearly visible and easily accessible.

content.subtitle: Cancellation by Businesses and Educational Institutions
content.text: For businesses and educational institutions, the terms and cancellation periods agreed upon in the respective contract apply. Generally, the cancellation notice period is three months to the end of the term unless otherwise agreed. Billing contracts can be canceled with 30 days' notice.

content.subtitle: Extraordinary Cancellation
content.text: The right to extraordinary cancellation (termination without notice) for good cause remains unaffected (§ 314 German Civil Code). Good cause exists in particular for: (a) serious violations of these Terms; (b) payment default for more than two months; (c) technical app failure lasting longer than 30 days; (d) violation of data protection law; (e) non-performance of essential services.

content.subtitle: Consequences of Cancellation
content.text: Upon cancellation, access to the app ends at the end of the billing period or at the time of extraordinary cancellation. Fees already paid for future services are refunded proportionally if the service is no longer provided. For lifetime access, there is no refund after cancellation. User data will be deleted after 90 days following cancellation, unless legal retention obligations prevent this.

content.subtitle: Support Channels
content.text: BeAFox offers support through the following channels: (1) Email: info@beafox.app; (2) In-app support/chat (available in settings); (3) Contact form on the website: www.beafox.app/kontakt

content.subtitle: Support Hours
content.text: Support is provided on business days (Monday to Friday) from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CET. Inquiries outside these hours will be processed on the next business day. Support is closed on German public holidays.

content.subtitle: Response Time
content.text: BeAFox endeavors to respond to inquiries quickly. A typical response time is 24-48 hours. However, BeAFox makes no guarantee for a specific response time. There is no claim for immediate or real-time feedback.

content.subtitle: Scope of Support
content.text: Standard support includes: (a) technical questions about app use; (b) assistance with login and access issues; (c) general questions about subscriptions and cancellation; (d) reporting bugs and technical errors. Not included are: (a) individual financial advice; (b) training; (c) custom development; (d) consulting. These services can be booked separately.

content.subtitle: Ticketing System
content.text: Support requests are managed in a ticketing system. Customers receive a confirmation email with a ticket number.

content.subtitle: Workshop Agreements
content.text: Workshops are conducted based on individual arrangements. The details (date, time, duration, content, number of participants, format: online or on-site) are specified in a separate offer or contract.

content.subtitle: Additional Costs
content.text: Travel costs, material costs, accommodation costs, catering, and other expenses are charged separately unless otherwise agreed in the offer. These costs will be communicated transparently in advance.

content.subtitle: Cancellation Provisions
content.text: If a workshop is canceled by BeAFox, an alternative date will be offered or the fee already paid will be refunded. If canceled by the customer (e.g., failure to register participants), agreed cancellation terms apply.

content.subtitle: Workshop Cancellation Terms
content.text: Cancellations of workshops must be made in writing. Cancellation periods: (1) Cancellation at least 14 days before the date: full refund minus processing fee (10%); (2) Cancellation 7-14 days before the date: 50% refund; (3) Cancellation less than 7 days before the date or non-attendance: no refund.

content.subtitle: Certificates and Materials
content.text: Certificates and printed teaching materials are provided after workshop completion. Printing and shipping costs are included in the fee unless otherwise agreed. Digital materials are sent by email.

content.subtitle: Monitoring Dashboards
content.text: For educational institutions and businesses, monitoring dashboards with usage and performance data are provided. The scope and update frequency will be agreed upon in the respective contract.

content.subtitle: Bea - AI-Powered Assistant
content.text: "Bea" is an AI-powered assistant that helps users with questions about financial education. Bea is NOT a real person and is powered by Artificial Intelligence (Large Language Models). Users should be aware that they are communicating with an automated system.

content.subtitle: Transparency Labeling
content.text: BeAFox clearly identifies Bea as an AI system. Interactions with Bea take place with full understanding that these are automated contents, not human advice or personal recommendations.

content.subtitle: AI-Generated Content
content.text: Content generated by Bea may occasionally contain errors, inaccuracies, or outdated information. BeAFox reviews AI content but cannot guarantee error-free content. Users should verify important information through other sources.

content.subtitle: No Personal Advice
content.text: Bea does NOT provide personal financial advice, tax advice, or legal advice. Bea provides general information and learning materials. For personal advice, users should consult a qualified professional (tax advisor, financial advisor, etc.).

content.subtitle: Data Protection in AI Use
content.text: Conversations with Bea are recorded and used to improve the AI system (in accordance with the Privacy Policy). Users should not enter sensitive personal data (account numbers, SSN, etc.) in conversations with Bea. BeAFox processes this data in accordance with the GDPR and TDDDG.

content.subtitle: EU AI Act
content.text: BeAFox aligns with the requirements of the EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689, when in effect) regarding transparency, risk mitigation, and documentation of AI systems.

content.subtitle: Right to Explanation
content.text: Users have the right to request explanations for decisions made by AI systems (Article 22 GDPR). BeAFox will provide explanations in response to reasonable requests.

sections.title: Online Dispute Resolution and Consumer Dispute Resolution

content.subtitle: EU Online Dispute Resolution Platform
content.text:
The European Commission has established a platform for out-of-court online dispute resolution (ODR platform). Consumers can use these free platforms to resolve disputes:

URL: https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/

BeAFox is willing to participate in proceedings on this platform.

content.subtitle: Consumer Arbitration Centers
content.text: In Germany, there are consumer arbitration centers (e.g., Arbitration Center of the Retail Trade Association). These can be called upon to resolve disputes. BeAFox will consider appropriate arbitration requests but is not required to participate under the German Consumer Dispute Resolution Act (VSBG).

content.subtitle: Notice pursuant to VSBG
content.text: Pursuant to § 36 VSBG, we notify: BeAFox UG (haftungsbeschränkt) is not obligated and not willing to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration center. However, out-of-court dispute resolution is possible if both parties agree.

content.subtitle: Court Proceedings
content.text: Should a dispute not be resolved out-of-court, consumers can contact the appropriate courts. For consumers, the statutory court jurisdiction rules apply. For businesses, the place of jurisdiction is the District Court (Amtsgericht) or Regional Court (Landgericht) Regensburg, where BeAFox is located.

content.subtitle: Right to Change
content.text: BeAFox reserves the right to amend these Terms so that they always comply with current legal requirements, reflect changes in services, or implement technical innovations. Changes may also be required to comply with German and European law requirements (e.g., new consumer protection laws).

content.subtitle: Notification of Changes
content.text: Users will be informed of material changes to the Terms at least two weeks before they take effect. Notification is made via email, in-app notification, or on the website. Non-material changes (e.g., contact information) can be made without prior notice.

content.subtitle: Explicit Consent Required for Material Changes
content.text: Explicit user consent is required for material changes (price increases, service restrictions, liability exclusions). Material changes require: (1) written notification at least two weeks in advance; (2) option to object or cancel; (3) silence does NOT constitute consent.

content.subtitle: Right to Cancel for Non-Acceptance
content.text: If a user does not agree to amended Terms, they can cancel the contract in writing by the effective date of the changes. After cancellation, the old contract remains valid until the end of the current billing period. No further renewal will occur.

content.subtitle: Consent to Non-Material Changes
content.text: For non-material changes (editorial adjustments, clarifications, technical improvements), consent is obtained through continued use of the app after notice on the website.

content.subtitle: No Tacit Consent
content.text: Silence or failure to object does NOT constitute consent to amended Terms. BeAFox will explicitly request consent for each change.

BeAFox is entitled to assign rights and obligations under this contract in whole or in part to a successor (e.g., in the event of merger, acquisition, or restructuring) or other third parties, provided this is reasonable for the customer and the customer has the opportunity to cancel the contract.

content.subtitle: Notification and Right to Object
content.text: The customer will be notified in writing of an intended assignment at least three weeks in advance. The customer has the right to cancel the contract by the time of assignment if the assignment is unreasonable for the customer or would result in significant disadvantages.

content.subtitle: Qualification of Successor
content.text: A successor must be able to perform the services under the same or better terms. The customer's rights (including warranty and damage claims) remain unaffected.

content.subtitle: Prohibition of Assignment by Customers
content.text: Customers are not permitted to assign their rights or obligations under this contract to third parties without prior written consent from BeAFox. This applies in particular to the sharing of access credentials.

content.subtitle: Applicable Law
content.text: German law (Federal Republic of Germany) applies to the exclusion of the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG) and International Private Law (IPR), insofar as these laws are not mandatory (e.g., consumer protection laws).

content.subtitle: Jurisdiction
content.text: The place of jurisdiction for all disputes arising from this contract is the District Court (Amtsgericht) or Regional Court (Landgericht) Regensburg if the customer is a merchant (within the meaning of the German Commercial Code), a legal entity of public law, or a publicly-owned special fund. For consumers, the statutory court jurisdiction rules apply under §§ 32 et seq. German Code of Civil Procedure (consumers can also sue BeAFox, and BeAFox can sue the consumer).

content.subtitle: Severability Clause
content.text: Should any provision of these Terms be wholly or partly invalid or become invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions remains unaffected. The invalid provision does not apply. For the invalid provision, the statutory regulations of the Federal Republic of Germany apply. This does not apply if the severability clause itself would be invalid.

content.subtitle: Entire Agreement
content.text: These Terms, together with the offer and Privacy Policy, constitute the entire agreement between BeAFox and the customer. Earlier statements, agreements, or contracts are superseded by these Terms unless expressly otherwise agreed.

content.subtitle: Changes Require Written Form
content.text: Amendments or supplements to these Terms require written form (in writing by mail, fax, or email). This also applies to a waiver of this provision (§ 127 German Civil Code).

content.subtitle: Contract Language
content.text: These Terms are drafted in the German language and constitute the binding version. Translations into other languages serve for information purposes only and are not binding. In case of discrepancies between the German and a translated version, the German version shall prevail.

items.question: Are the BeAFox calculators really free?
items.answer: Yes. All calculators on BeAFox are completely free and usable without signup. We do not make money with ads or by selling your data.

items.question: How up to date are the tax rates and values?
items.answer: The calculators are based on the current values for 2026 - minimum wage, basic allowance, social security contributions, and all relevant tax rates. We update the values every year.

items.question: Does BeAFox store my entered data?
items.answer: No. All calculations happen directly in your browser. We do not store any inputs and do not track personal data.

items.question: Do I need an account to use the calculators?
items.answer: No. You can use all calculators directly in the browser without logging in. Only if you want to use Bea as your personal finance companion in the app do you need a free account.

hero.subtitle: Learn more about our journey, events, updates, and how we’re revolutionizing financial education for young people.

specialMomentsSection.subtitle: The most important milestones of our journey with BeAFox.

allArticlesSection.subtitle: Explore all our blog posts and updates.

specialMoments.title: We won!
specialMoments.description: We’re proud to have won the Deggendorfer Gründerpreis! Despite nerves and a last-minute pitch update, our work paid off—around 60% of the audience voted for us. Thanks to the whole team for the great organization and the €2,500 prize. We’re excited for the next steps with BeAFox!

specialMoments.title: BeAFox at Ed.One in Munich!
specialMoments.description: This is what financial education feels like in the classroom. At the Ed.One Summit in Munich, students, teachers, and trainers tested BeAFox live—from the dashboard to the learning app and worksheets. We received valuable feedback, made exciting connections, and presented our pitch. BeAFox shows how simple financial education can be. We’re looking for you!

specialMoments.title: BeAFox wins Startup Summit!
specialMoments.description: BeAFox achieved 2nd place at Startup Summit Germany—our first official award! For the first time, we pitched as a duo with a new pitch, deck, and an even clearer vision. After two years of hard work, it shows that perseverance, courage, and teamwork pay off. Thanks to Volksbank am Württemberg eG for this great event!

articles.title: Startchancen program
articles.description: Big news: BeAFox is part of the Startchancen program! This allows schools to fully fund our learning app through subsidies. For us, it’s a strong signal that financial education belongs in everyday school life. Many of us would have loved an offer like this in school—practical, playful, and relevant. Now we can reach schools that previously had little access to such opportunities, bringing us closer to real equal opportunities.

articles.title: Rütteltest with the Economic Junior Chamber Regensburg
articles.description: At the Rütteltest of the Wirtschaftsjunioren Regensburg we pitched BeAFox for over 20 minutes and then received more than an hour of honest, in-depth feedback from six experts. Our entire business model was constructively challenged—exactly what we wanted. We’re taking away many impulses, set new priorities, and decided to join the organization right away. An experience we recommend to any startup.

articles.title: Our first workshop.
articles.description: Our first financial education workshop for apprentices—combined with the BeAFox app—was a great success. Instead of one-off theory, we focus on practical exercises: building personal budgets, opening a brokerage account, planning first investments, understanding insurance, and using a tax return. The best part: with BeAFox, participants can keep learning after the workshop and deepen their knowledge. Companies get a sustainable education offering.

articles.title: BeAFox at DemoNight in Nuremberg
articles.description: BeAFox at BayStartup’s DemoNight in Nuremberg: right when we arrived, a founder approached us who knew us from social media—a small moment that motivated us a lot. The evening was full of exciting conversations, new contacts, and innovative ideas. Events like this remind us why we build BeAFox: to solve real problems and bring financial education to where it truly matters in young people’s everyday lives.

articles.title: What an experience: YFC
articles.description: What an experience: we attended YFC in Munich with BeAFox, the first conference of the Young Founders Network. Between inspiring speakers, many startups, and great networking, we showcased our learning app at our own booth. The organization, atmosphere, and conversations impressed us. A special highlight: the announcement of the Young Founders Fund—a chance we definitely want to pursue.

articles.title: BeAFox at the Summit of the Entrepreneurship Foundation
articles.description: BeAFox at the Summit of the Stiftung Entrepreneurship in Berlin—an inspiring weekend. Again and again we heard: “Financial education is important!” and felt how big the need for modern solutions is. We presented our app, received consistently positive feedback, and had intense conversations with people who take the topic as seriously as we do. This event strengthened our vision even more.

articles.title: BeAFox at the BÖB Congress
articles.description: At the congress of Bündnis Ökonomische Bildung Deutschland, there was a lot of discussion about making financial education a standalone school subject—with new textbooks, courses, and training. I asked: is that really enough? With teacher shortages and a topic many people avoid talking about? I believe we also need new paths: digital tools, gamification, and project-based learning. That’s exactly where BeAFox comes in—as a complement, not a competitor to teaching.

articles.title: We did it
articles.description: With BeAFox we achieved 2nd place at Startup Summit Germany! Our first team pitch together, a new deck, a new concept—and an award including prize money. After two intense years of testing, discarding, and improving, this moment feels like a strong sign: our idea works. A huge thank you to everyone supporting us and to Volksbank am Württemberg eG.

articles.title: I pitched BeAFox at Innovation Day
articles.description: My first pitch in English—five minutes at Innovation Day in front of an international audience. Even though we didn’t win this time, it was an incredibly valuable experience. I presented BeAFox on a new stage, gained new perspectives on financial education, and made many exciting connections. Moments like these show how important it is to be brave, try new things, and continuously improve.

articles.title: BeAFox at ChamlandSchau
articles.description: Left school at 18, no plan for life, and built up debt—that’s how my story began. Today I’ve learned how to handle money and built a startup with BeAFox that tackles exactly that. At ChamlandSchau, our small booth stood among big companies—and still we had nonstop conversations. It shows: young people feel that financial education is missing and are looking for answers.

articles.title: Release in App Store & Play Store
articles.description: It’s happening: BeAFox is officially available in the App Store and Play Store! After more than 1.5 years of development, testing, and countless adjustments, we went live—three months earlier than planned. Our goal: make financial education as simple and accessible as possible, without ads or hidden sales interests. Now every download, every feedback, and every recommendation helps us reach more young people.

articles.title: Donau-Post article
articles.description: BeAFox in the newspaper—this time with a very special headline: “We need to talk about money!” Donau-Post published an in-depth article about us and how an idea became a real learning offer for schools. After 1.5 years of work, doubts, and many late nights, this moment is special. It shows that the time invested pays off—and our mission is reaching more and more people.

articles.title: A new logo for BeAFox
articles.description: Feedback is gold: our first logo felt improvised and not professional enough—so we acted. Together with a designer, we created a new BeAFox logo: more modern, clear, friendly, and perfectly aligned with our target group. For us this is more than a design update. It’s a symbol of how seriously we take our mission and how important a consistent brand presence is for schools, companies, and users.

articles.title: Mittelbayerische Zeitung
articles.description: “From smartphone to finance fox” — that was the headline of our first major article in Mittelbayerische Zeitung. A goosebump moment to see our vision in print. Focus: why financial education is so important for young people, how BeAFox supports teachers in class, and why we intentionally stay independent from ads and sales interests. A huge thank you to everyone involved!

articles.title: BeAFox in the classroom for the first time
articles.description: Today was special: BeAFox was used in real class for the first time—at the State Secondary School Neutraubling. Together with 25 students we tried financial education with the app. Result: 24 out of 25 want BeAFox to be a fixed part of their lessons. For us, a strong signal that young people want digital, interactive formats—and that our approach fits the classroom.

articles.title: Another successful pitch for BeAFox
articles.description: At Ideenpitch Region Nürnberg we presented BeAFox outside Regensburg for the first time. The improved pitch was well received and helped make our idea clearer and more tangible. We especially enjoyed the many conversations with people from education and business. Our goal: bring BeAFox to schools in time for the Bavarian school start—so financial education finally arrives where it belongs.

articles.title: My first pitch — in front of 200+ people
articles.description: My very first pitch—and straight away in front of more than 200 people at the “Tigers” of TechBase Regensburg! I presented our BeAFox vision, why financial education is so important for young people, and how our app addresses that gap. The conversations afterward were especially valuable: teachers, students, and companies recognized themselves in the story and showed interest in collaborating.

articles.title: It’s official. We went to the notary
articles.description: BeAFox is officially founded: we went to the notary and signed the articles of association—a major milestone after months of ideas, feedback, and testing. Our goal remains clear: make financial education understandable, independent, and playful for young people. Next up: trademark registration, more pilot projects with schools and companies, and the next big step toward a breakthrough.

articles.title: What a day: DENK.Summit
articles.description: With BeAFox we presented our startup in the startup area of DENK.Summit—with overwhelmingly positive feedback: “insanely beautiful booth”, “cool branding, you really stand out”. In just two hours, 50+ pupils and students tested the app. Almost everyone confirmed: financial education barely happens in schools today. This experience once again showed us how important our mission is.

meta.title: Community Guidelines | BeAFox
meta.description: Comprehensive community guidelines for a safe, legal, and respectful learning environment. DSA-compliant with youth protection and transparent moderation.

hero.description: BeAFox is a community for young people aged 16 to 30 who together acquire financial capability. These guidelines create a safe, respectful space that protects all users, ensures compliance with European and German laws, and maintains the quality of our learning environment. They are based on the EU Digital Services Act (DSA), the Youth Protection Act (JuSchG), the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), and other applicable legal standards.

items.title: Respectful Behavior
items.description: Treat all members of the BeAFox community with respect and kindness. A positive culture of mutual trust is the foundation of our learning community.
rules: Be respectful and courteous in all interactions – online and offline
Avoid insulting, discriminatory, racist, sexist, or demeaning language or behavior
Respect different opinions, religions, identities, and experiences
Help others learn instead of criticizing or shaming them
Celebrate others' successes and encourage them constructively in their learning journey

items.title: Constructive Communication
items.description: Share your knowledge constructively and help others learn. Open, factual discussions promote understanding for everyone.
rules: Ask questions when you don't understand something, and make an effort to understand answers
Share your experiences and tips factually and evidence-based with the community
Provide constructive feedback that concretely helps others improve
Avoid spam, advertising, unsolicited promotions, or unwanted commercialized content
Keep discussions factual, on-topic, and free of personal attacks

items.title: Prohibited Content (DSA Art. 14, NetzDG)
items.description: The following content is absolutely not permitted in the BeAFox community. These restrictions comply with the EU Digital Services Act and the German Network Enforcement Act.
rules: Hate content and incitement: Content that incites violence, discrimination, or hatred against persons based on race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or other protected characteristics (§ 130, § 131 StGB)
Violence and threats: Threats or glorification of violence, suicide, or self-harm; content that intimidates or threatens others
Harassment and doxxing: Sustained or repeated harassment of persons; disclosure of private personal data (addresses, phone numbers, etc.) without consent

Sexual and child safety content: Child sexual abuse material (CSAM) or any form of sexual abuse or exploitation of children; content offering sexual services or sexualized content involving minors (§§ 176, 182, 184 StGB)
Terrorism and extremism: Content that glorifies, spreads, or incites terrorism; propaganda material of extremist organizations
Fraud and illegal activities: Content inciting or instructing fraud, document forgery, theft, or other crimes; distribution of malware or phishing links
Copyright and intellectual property: Distribution of content that violates third-party copyrights, trademarks, or other intellectual property rights without legal basis

Impersonation and deception: Impersonating another person's identity, especially BeAFox staff, moderators, or public figures; spreading deliberately false or misleading information about financial topics
Illegally offered goods or services: Distribution or promotion of unlawfully manufactured, imported, or distributed goods

items.title: Security and Privacy
items.description: Protect your personal data and that of other users. Trust and data security are essential for our community. BeAFox is GDPR-compliant and protects all user data strictly.
rules: Do not share personal financial data such as account numbers, PINs, TAN numbers, credit card data, or other sensitive information in public areas
Never share passwords, access credentials, or authentication codes – BeAFox staff will never ask you for these
Respect other users' privacy: Do not share their personal data, images, or private information without explicit consent
Report suspicious activities, phishing attempts, unusual account access, or security vulnerabilities immediately to support@beafox.app
Use a strong, unique password for your BeAFox account and enable two-factor authentication if available
Understand that BeAFox processes your data in accordance with GDPR and our privacy policy – find privacy information at beafox.app/privacy

items.title: Youth Protection and Protection of Minors (JuSchG, JMStV)
items.description: BeAFox especially protects users under 18 years of age. We strictly follow the German Youth Protection Act (JuSchG) and Interstate Treaty on the Protection of Minors in the Media (JMStV).
rules: Minor users (under 18): Their safety is the highest priority. Content is always curated to be age-appropriate and educational

No contact by adults for private purposes: Adults must not build relationships with minors outside of open learning contexts. Any suspicious or inappropriate contact will be reported and may result in criminal consequences
Protection mechanisms for minors: BeAFox uses content filtering, age verification, and moderation to protect minors from harmful content
Use of appropriate language and content: All public content must be appropriate for users aged 16 and above. Alcohol, drugs, smoking, and explicitly sexual content are not permitted
Reporting: Any suspicious activity regarding child welfare risks will be reported to police and/or competent authorities (in accordance with § 8a SGB VIII and § 14 Para. 2 JMStV)

items.title: No Financial Advice
items.description: BeAFox is a platform for financial education, not financial advice or investment advice. Bea is an AI-powered learning companion, not a licensed financial advisor.
rules: BeAFox conveys financial knowledge and capability but not personal or legally binding financial advice
Do not give specific investment recommendations, tips, or financial forecasts – that is financial advice, which requires a license
Refer to professional, licensed financial advisors or authorities (such as BaFin) for complex or personal financial questions
Share general financial knowledge and concepts, but not your personal financial decisions or strategies that could be misunderstood as advice
Remind others that individual professional advice is important and recommended for major financial decisions (loans, investments, insurance)

items.title: Artificial Intelligence and Bea – Transparency and Limitations
items.description: Bea is our AI-powered learning companion. It is important that you understand how Bea works, what Bea can do, and what Bea cannot do.
rules: Bea is an AI: Bea is powered by artificial intelligence and can make mistakes. Bea is not human and has no personal feelings or intentions
Transparency about AI-generated content: Content generated by Bea or other AI systems will be labeled as such. BeAFox does not secretly mix AI content with human content

Bea's limitations: Bea does not provide professional financial advice, cannot support during crisis situations, and does not replace human experts. For serious problems, contact a trusted person, therapist, or authorities
Data privacy with AI: Your conversations with Bea are used to train and improve Bea (anonymously and pseudonymously in accordance with GDPR). Detailed information can be found at beafox.app/privacy
Complaints about Bea behavior: If Bea content seems inappropriate or misrepresents you, you can report this. BeAFox will review the case

items.title: Reporting Illegal Content (DSA Art. 16 – Notice and Action)
items.description: The EU Digital Services Act requires us to have a structured process for reporting illegal content. You can report content that violates laws or these guidelines.
rules: Reporting channel: Use the report form in the app under Settings > Report Content or write directly to compliance@beafox.app. Provide the content URL or screenshot plus description

Required information for a report: Concrete description of the content, why it is illegal or violates guidelines, link or screenshot, your contact details (optional but helpful for follow-up). Factual and detailed formulation helps us act quickly

Processing of illegal content: Content that violates applicable German or EU law (e.g., CSAM, terrorism, incitement to violence) will be reviewed within 72 hours and removed if confirmed illegal. Guideline violations will be processed within 7 days
Report status tracking: You receive confirmation of your report and can track its status (in the app under Reports or by email)
Appeal against incorrect report: If a report is handled incorrectly, the user can appeal (see Point 10: Complaint Management)

items.title: Content Moderation and Decision Transparency (DSA Art. 14, 17)
items.description: BeAFox moderates content to enforce these guidelines. We are transparent about how, why, and by whom decisions are made.
rules: Moderation methods: BeAFox uses a combination of automated detection (artificial intelligence) and human review. Flagged content is reviewed by trained moderators before action is taken

Duty to provide reasons (DSA Art. 17): If your account is suspended, a post is deleted, or other action is taken, you receive notification with: (1) the exact guideline section or reason, (2) the specific justification for why the action was taken, (3) information about your right to complain. This information will be provided within reasonable time
Transparency report: BeAFox publishes regular reports on content moderation: number of reports received, content processed, reasons for deletions, and complaints
Human review for complaints: If you appeal a moderation decision, your case is reviewed again by a different trained moderator (not the same person, not just automated systems)
Emergency exceptions: In case of imminent danger (e.g., suicide announcement, bomb threat), BeAFox can remove content without warning and contact authorities

items.title: Complaint Management and Remedies (DSA Art. 20)
items.description: You have the right to lodge complaints against moderation decisions, suspensions, or deletions. BeAFox offers an internal review process and external options.
rules: Internal complaint: If you wish to contest a moderation decision, you can submit a complaint to appeals@beafox.app within 14 days of notification. Specify: (1) which decision you are contesting, (2) your reasons why the decision is wrong, (3) if applicable, new information. Format factually and specifically
Internal review period: BeAFox reviews your complaint within 14 days and informs you of the result: decision upheld, partially reversed, or fully overturned. The reasoning will be provided

Out-of-court dispute resolution: If you disagree with the internal decision, you can initiate proceedings with an independent ombudsman or arbitration. BeAFox is a member of the Digital Services Act complaint procedure (details at beafox.app/complaints)
Judicial review: You have the right to contest any moderation decision before a German or EU court (administrative law or private law depending on the situation)
No retaliation: BeAFox will not suspend your account, delete your content, or otherwise harm you for filing a complaint. That is retaliation and is prohibited

violations.title: Violations of Guidelines
violations.description: BeAFox enforces these guidelines to protect all users. Depending on severity and frequency, the following measures may be taken. All measures will be communicated transparently with justification and right to complain.
actions: Level 1 – Warning: The user is informed that a violation may have occurred and is asked to change their behavior. The content may be hidden but not deleted
Level 2 – Content Removal: The violating content is removed. The user is notified with justification and can file a complaint

Level 3 – Temporary Suspension (7–90 days): The user account is suspended for a defined period. The user cannot access the platform during this time. All other functions (lessons, quizzes, rankings) are suspended. Conversations are possible after suspension is lifted

Level 4 – Permanent Suspension: The user account is permanently and irrevocably deleted for repeated serious violations, legal violations (CSAM, terrorism), or violations endangering others' safety. The user can no longer access the platform. BeAFox may retain data for prosecution or civil claims

Level 5 – Report to Authorities: If there is suspicion of criminal activity (child abuse, terrorism, fraud, harassment), BeAFox will notify the competent German or European authorities (police, BKA, NCMEC, Europol) and may provide evidence
Special considerations for minors: For violations by or against minors, BeAFox is guided by JuSchG and JMStV. In case of welfare risks, the youth welfare office may be contacted

Repetition and escalation: First violations result in warnings. Repeated violations result in harsher measures (suspension, deletion). Serious violations (e.g., hate, violence, CSAM, fraud) result directly in suspension and/or authority reporting, regardless of whether there was an initial warning
