hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.description: We take the protection of your personal data very seriously. This privacy policy informs you about the type, scope and purpose of processing personal data in accordance with GDPR, TDDDG (Telecommunications Digital Services Data Protection Act) and DDG (Digital Services Act).

sections.title: Information about the collection of personal data

content.subtitle: General
content.text: Below we inform you about the collection of personal data when using this website and our app. Personal data is all data that can be related to you personally, such as name, address, email addresses, user behavior.

content.subtitle: Responsible party
content.text: The responsible party within the meaning of GDPR, service provider within the meaning of TDDDG and telecommunications service provider within the meaning of DDG is:
details: Trade Register: HRB 21689, Registration Court Regensburg

content.subtitle: Data Protection Officer
content.text: We currently have not appointed a data protection officer, as this is not required under the applicable legal provisions.

sections.title: Your rights as a data subject

content.subtitle: Right to information (Art. 15 GDPR)
content.text: You have the right to request information about your personal data processed by us. In particular, you can request information about:
list: the data stored about you with us
recipients or categories of recipients to whom this data is passed on

content.subtitle: Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR)
content.text: You have the right to request correction of inaccurate or completion of incomplete personal data stored with us.

content.subtitle: Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR)
content.text: You have the right to request deletion of your personal data, unless statutory retention obligations or other legal grounds prevent deletion.

content.subtitle: Right to restrict processing (Art. 18 GDPR)
content.text: You have the right to request restriction of processing of your personal data.

content.subtitle: Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)
content.text: You have the right to receive the personal data you have provided to us in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format.

content.subtitle: Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR)
content.text: You have the right to object at any time to the processing of personal data concerning you on grounds relating to your particular situation, provided such processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point e or f GDPR.

content.subtitle: Withdrawal of consent
content.text: If you have given consent to the processing of your personal data, you can withdraw this consent at any time. The lawfulness of processing carried out until withdrawal is not affected. Please direct all information requests, data access requests or objections regarding data processing to info@beafox.app or to the address stated above.

content.subtitle: Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (Art. 77 GDPR)
content.text: You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority regarding the processing of your personal data. The competent authority is generally the supervisory authority of your usual place of residence, your place of work or the place of the alleged infringement.
details: The competent authority for us is:
Bavarian State Office for Data Protection Supervision (BayLDA)

content.text: We maintain current technical measures to ensure data security, in particular to protect your personal data against dangers during data transmission and against access by third parties. These are continuously adapted to the current state of technology.

content.subtitle: TLS encryption
content.text: We use TLS encryption for the transmission of content (recognizable by "https://" and the padlock symbol in the browser). This protects your data against unauthorized interception.

content.subtitle: Hosting
content.text: Our website is hosted by Vercel Inc., 340 S Lemon Ave #4133, Walnut, CA 91789, USA. We have concluded a data processing agreement with the host in accordance with Art. 28 GDPR.

content.subtitle: Server log files
content.text: When you access our website, our host automatically records data and information in so-called server log files. This includes in particular:
list: Time zone difference to Greenwich Mean Time (GMT)
Referrer URL (website from which the request originates)
Language and version of browser software

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: Processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point f GDPR. Our legitimate interest lies in the technical provision of the website, ensuring stability and security, and investigating and preventing misuse or fraudulent activity.

content.subtitle: Storage duration
content.text: Server log files are stored for 7 days and then deleted.

content.subtitle: What are cookies?
content.text: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your hard drive and associated with the browser you use, through which the party setting the cookie (in this case us) receives certain information. Cookies cannot execute programs or transmit viruses to your computer. They are used to make the internet offer more user-friendly and effective overall.

content.subtitle: Use of cookies
content.text: We use cookies to identify you on subsequent visits if you have an account with us. Otherwise, you would have to log in again for each visit.

content.subtitle: Cookie types
content.text: This website uses cookies as follows:
list: Transient cookies (temporary use): Transient cookies are automatically deleted when you close the browser. These include in particular session cookies. These store a so-called session ID, which allows different requests from your browser to be assigned to the shared session. This allows your computer to be recognized when you return to the website. Session cookies are deleted when you log out or close the browser.

content.subtitle: Consent via consent tool
content.text: Where we use non-technically necessary cookies (e.g. for statistics or marketing purposes), we first obtain your consent through a consent management tool. We currently do not use cookies for analysis or marketing purposes on this website.

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: The storage of information on your device or access to it is, unless absolutely necessary, based on your consent (§ 25 para. 1 TDDDG in conjunction with Art. 6 para. 1 point a GDPR). We use technically necessary cookies on the basis of § 25 para. 2 TDDDG and Art. 6 para. 1 point f GDPR.

content.subtitle: Data separation
content.text: This stored information is stored separately from any additional data you provide to us. In particular, cookie data is not linked with your other data.

content.subtitle: Contact form
content.text: If you send us inquiries via the contact form, your information from the inquiry form including the contact details you provide will be stored by us for the purpose of processing your inquiry and in case of follow-up questions. We do not share this data without your consent.

content.subtitle: Email communication
content.text: If you contact us by email, we store your email address and the information you provide in order to respond to your inquiry.

content.subtitle: Sending via SendGrid
content.text: We use the SendGrid service (Twilio SendGrid, Inc., 1801 California Street, Suite 500, Denver, CO 80202, USA) for sending emails. We have concluded a data processing agreement with the provider in accordance with Art. 28 GDPR. SendGrid processes the data in the USA. We have agreed Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) with SendGrid to ensure an adequate standard of data protection.

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: The legal basis for processing data from contact forms and emails is Art. 6 para. 1 point b GDPR, insofar as your inquiry is directed toward the conclusion or performance of a contract, and Art. 6 para. 1 point f GDPR otherwise. Our legitimate interest lies in processing inquiries.

content.subtitle: Storage duration
content.text: The data will be deleted 6 months after we have finished processing your inquiry, unless statutory retention obligations prevent deletion.

content.subtitle: Newsletter subscription
content.text: If you subscribe to our newsletter, we will use the email address you provide to send you regular information about BeAFox, updates and financial education tips.

content.subtitle: Sending via SendGrid
content.text: We use the SendGrid service (Twilio SendGrid, Inc., 1801 California Street, Suite 500, Denver, CO 80202, USA) for sending our newsletter. We have concluded a data processing agreement with the provider in accordance with Art. 28 GDPR. SendGrid processes the data in the USA. We have agreed Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) with SendGrid.

content.subtitle: Registration procedure
content.text: Registration takes place using the double opt-in procedure. After registration, you will receive an email asking you to confirm your subscription. Only after this confirmation will your email address be activated for newsletter delivery.

content.subtitle: Logging of opt-in
content.text: Your consent and the date and time of registration are logged in order to be able to provide legal evidence for this.

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: The legal basis for sending the newsletter is your consent (Art. 6 para. 1 point a GDPR).

content.subtitle: Withdrawal
content.text: You can withdraw your consent to receive the newsletter at any time. Each newsletter email contains a corresponding link. You can also send an email to info@beafox.app at any time.

content.subtitle: Storage duration
content.text: Your newsletter data will be stored until you withdraw your consent.

sections.title: Use of the BeAFox app / creating a user account

content.subtitle: Registration
content.text: When registering for a user account in the BeAFox app, we collect the following data:

content.subtitle: Purpose of processing
content.text: This data is needed to provide you with access to the app, manage your user account and offer you personalized learning content.

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: Processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point b GDPR to fulfill the terms of use.

content.subtitle: Storage duration
content.text: Your account data will be stored as long as your user account exists. After deletion of your account, the data will be deleted unless statutory retention obligations prevent deletion. Individual data (e.g. for billing purposes) may be retained for an additional 30 days.

sections.title: Processing of learning and usage data

content.subtitle: Collected data
content.text: When using the BeAFox app, we process the following data:
list: Learning progress (completed lessons, missions, XP points)
Device information (device type, operating system, app version)

content.subtitle: Purpose of processing
content.text: This data is used to:
list: Save and display your learning progress

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: Processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point b GDPR to fulfill the terms of use as well as Art. 6 para. 1 point f GDPR to improve our services.

content.subtitle: Cloud storage
content.text: Your learning and usage data is stored on our servers to enable synchronization between different devices. This allows you to access your learning progress on any device.

content.subtitle: Storage duration
content.text: The data will be stored until deletion of your user account.

sections.title: In-app purchases and payments via app stores

content.subtitle: Payment processing
content.text: If you make in-app purchases in the BeAFox app (e.g. premium subscription), payment processing is handled by the respective app store (Apple App Store or Google Play Store).

content.subtitle: Processing by app stores
content.text: The app stores process the following data:

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: The processing of payment data is carried out by the app stores on the basis of their respective privacy policies. We only receive confirmations of successful payments, not payment data.

content.subtitle: Notice
content.text: Please note the privacy policies of Apple (https://www.apple.com/privacy/) and Google (https://policies.google.com/privacy) for further information on how your payment data is processed.

content.subtitle: Payment processing
content.text: On our website we offer paid subscriptions through a checkout. Payment processing is handled by Stripe Inc., 354 Oyster Point Blvd, South San Francisco, CA 94080, USA. We offer the following plans:

content.subtitle: Data processing by Stripe
content.text: During payment processing, Stripe processes the following data: payment method information (credit card, SEPA direct debit, etc.), billing address, email address, transaction details. We have concluded a data processing agreement with Stripe in accordance with Art. 28 GDPR and have agreed to Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC).

content.subtitle: BeAFox only receives confirmations
content.text: BeAFox does not process complete payment data. We only receive confirmation of successful payments and billing information for billing purposes.

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: Processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point b GDPR to fulfill the contract (provision of paid services).

content.subtitle: Data protection at Stripe
content.text: For further information on data processing by Stripe, please see Stripe's privacy policy: https://stripe.com/privacy

content.subtitle: Purpose
content.text: We may send you push notifications to inform you about new learning content, missions, leaderboard updates or important app updates.

content.subtitle: Consent
content.text: Push notifications are only sent with your explicit consent. You can disable these at any time in your app settings.

content.subtitle: Technical implementation
content.text: To send push notifications, we use the services of the respective platform operators (Apple Push Notification Service or Firebase Cloud Messaging from Google).

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: Processing is carried out on the basis of your consent (Art. 6 para. 1 point a GDPR).

content.subtitle: Google Analytics (GA4)
content.text: We use Google Analytics, a web analytics tool from Google LLC (1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA). Google Analytics processes data about your user behavior on our website using cookies. The collected data is used to analyze the use of our website, create reports and to help us improve our website. The ID of our Google Analytics property is: G-J0GWX92CNH.

content.subtitle: Consent for Google Analytics
content.text: Google Analytics is only used with your consent. You can grant or withdraw this consent through our consent management tool (cookie banner).

content.subtitle: IP address anonymization
content.text: We have configured Google Analytics to anonymize IP addresses before they are transmitted to Google.

content.subtitle: Standard Contractual Clauses
content.text: For data transmission to the USA, we have agreed Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) with Google.

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: Processing is carried out on the basis of your consent (Art. 6 para. 1 point a GDPR in conjunction with § 25 para. 1 TDDDG).

content.subtitle: Data protection at Google Analytics
content.text: For further information on data processing by Google Analytics, please visit: https://policies.google.com/privacy

content.subtitle: Ahrefs Analytics
content.text: We use Ahrefs Analytics (Ahrefs Pte. Ltd., 11 Changi Business Park, Singapore 486048), a web analytics tool for analyzing website traffic and search engine performance. Ahrefs processes pseudonymous data about your user behavior using cookies.

content.subtitle: Consent for Ahrefs
content.text: Ahrefs is only used with your consent. You can grant or withdraw this consent through our cookie consent tool.

content.subtitle: Legal basis for Ahrefs
content.text: Processing is carried out on the basis of your consent (Art. 6 para. 1 point a GDPR in conjunction with § 25 para. 1 TDDDG).

content.subtitle: Vercel Analytics (Web Vitals)
content.text: Additionally, we use Vercel Analytics from Vercel Inc. (340 S Lemon Ave #4133, Walnut, CA 91789, USA). Vercel Analytics is a privacy-friendly analytics tool that does not set cookies and does not collect personal data. It only measures technical metrics such as page load times and Core Web Vitals.

content.subtitle: No consent required for Vercel Analytics
content.text: Since Vercel Analytics does not process personal data and does not set cookies, no separate consent is required.

content.subtitle: Vercel Speed Insights
content.text: We also use Vercel Speed Insights from Vercel Inc. to measure website performance. This tool is also privacy-friendly, does not set cookies and does not collect personal data.

content.subtitle: Self-hosting without external connection
content.text: Our website uses fonts that are self-hosted via Next.js Font Optimization. The fonts are delivered directly from our servers and NOT loaded from Google servers. There is no connection to Google or external servers when the fonts are loaded.

content.subtitle: No IP transmission
content.text: Since the fonts are hosted locally, your IP address is not transmitted to Google or other third parties. This means increased data protection for you.

content.subtitle: No consent required
content.text: Since there is no connection to external servers, no special consent is required for the use of fonts.

content.subtitle: Bea - Your AI-based learning assistant
content.text: The BeAFox app offers an AI-powered learning assistant called "Bea". Bea uses artificial intelligence to provide you with personalized learning tips, answer questions and support your learning process.

content.subtitle: Data relationships in AI use
content.text: To improve response quality, Bea may analyze your learning and usage data (e.g. previous answers, learning progress). All data is processed within our systems.

content.subtitle: No profiling under Art. 22 GDPR
content.text: We refrain from automated decision-making through Bea. This means: Bea does NOT make legally binding decisions about you. Bea provides recommendations; the final decision is always yours.

content.subtitle: Transparency and human contact
content.text: If you are unsure about an AI-generated response or would like human review, please contact us at any time at info@beafox.app. Our team will review your request and provide you with a definitive answer.

content.subtitle: Legal basis
content.text: Processing by Bea is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point b GDPR (contract performance - provision of the AI assistant) and Art. 6 para. 1 point f GDPR (legitimate interest in improving services).

content.subtitle: EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689)
content.text: Our use of Bea complies with the EU Regulation on Artificial Intelligence (EU AI Act, Regulation 2024/1689). Bea is operated as a system of low to medium risk level. We are committed to transparency regarding the use of this technology.

content.subtitle: Links to other websites
content.text: Our website contains links to third-party websites. If you follow a link to one of these websites (which are outside our control), we would like to point out that these websites have their own privacy policies and that we are not responsible for and assume no liability for these policies. Please review these privacy policies before voluntarily providing personal data to these websites.

content.subtitle: Technical necessity
content.text: Only when you click on an external link will data be transmitted to the link destination. This is technically necessary due to the protocol underlying the Internet (TCP/IP). The data transmitted includes in particular: your IP address, the time you clicked the link, the page on which you clicked the link and the information mentioned above.

content.subtitle: Social media links
content.text: On our website you will find links to our social media profiles (Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok). If you click on these links, you will be directed to the respective platforms. Processing of your data will then take place in accordance with the privacy provisions of the respective platform.

content.subtitle: Consent (Art. 6 para. 1 point a GDPR)
content.text: If you have consented to the processing of your personal data, processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point a GDPR.

content.subtitle: Contract performance (Art. 6 para. 1 point b GDPR)
content.text: If processing is necessary to perform a contract to which you are a party, processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point b GDPR.

content.subtitle: Legal obligation (Art. 6 para. 1 point c GDPR)
content.text: If processing is necessary to comply with a legal obligation, processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point c GDPR.

content.subtitle: Legitimate interest (Art. 6 para. 1 point f GDPR)
content.text: Processing may also be carried out on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 point f GDPR if processing is necessary to protect a legitimate interest of our business or a third party and does not outweigh the interests, fundamental rights and fundamental freedoms of the data subject.

The criterion for the duration of storage of personal data is the respective statutory retention period. After the period expires, the corresponding data is routinely deleted unless it is still required for contract performance or contract initiation.

content.subtitle: Storage duration in detail
content.text: Below is an overview of the storage duration for individual data categories:
list: Contact inquiries: 6 months after completion of processing
Newsletter data: until withdrawal of consent
Account data: until deletion of account + 30 days (for billing)
Learning and usage data: until deletion of account
Payment data and receipts (Stripe): 10 years (in accordance with § 147 para. 3 AO and § 257 para. 4 HGB)

content.text: To the extent that no more specific storage period is mentioned in this privacy policy, your personal data will remain with us until the purpose for data processing ceases. If you assert a justified request for deletion or withdraw consent to data processing, your data will be deleted unless we have other legally valid reasons for storing your personal data.
As a responsible company, we refrain from automated decision-making or profiling. Your personal data will not be used for automated decision-making processes that could disadvantage you.

content.subtitle: Protection of minors
content.text: Our services are also directed at minors. We take the protection of the privacy of minors very seriously. According to Art. 8 GDPR, users under 16 years of age need the consent of their parents or guardians before we can process their personal data.

content.subtitle: Ensuring parental consent
content.text: We ensure that consents from minors under 16 years of age only take place with documented parental consent. During registration in the app, this is ensured by the following mechanisms:

list: For users under 16 years of age: display of a checkbox with the confirmation "I am over 16 years old or a parent/guardian has consented to this"
Sending a confirmation email to the parent's/guardian's email address
Access is only activated after confirmation of this email

content.subtitle: Parental control and data protection
content.text: Parents and guardians can request information about the data we store about their children and request deletion at any time. Please contact us at info@beafox.app for this purpose.

content.subtitle: No sensitive data
content.text: We intentionally do not process sensitive data according to Art. 9 GDPR (health data, religious data, data on criminal proceedings, etc.). Our app focuses exclusively on imparting financial knowledge to minors and adults.

content.subtitle: Compliance with Art. 8 GDPR
content.text: Our processes ensure full compliance with Art. 8 GDPR, in particular with regard to the consent of minors and the prevention of unauthorized data processing.

hero.title: Right of Withdrawal
hero.subtitle: Information about your statutory right of withdrawal under §§ 355 ff. BGB (German Civil Code) for distance contracts for digital content.

content.subtitle: Your right of withdrawal
content.text: You have the right to withdraw from this contract within fourteen days without giving any reason. The withdrawal period is fourteen days from the day of the conclusion of the contract.

content.subtitle: Exercising the right of withdrawal
content.text: To exercise your right of withdrawal, you must inform us (BeAFox UG (haftungsbeschränkt), Siemensweg 2, 93073 Neutraubling, Germany, email: info@beafox.app, phone: +49 178 2723 673) of your decision to withdraw from this contract by means of a clear statement (e.g. a letter sent by post or an email). You may use the attached model withdrawal form, but this is not mandatory.

content.subtitle: Meeting the deadline
content.text: To meet the withdrawal deadline, it is sufficient for you to send your communication concerning your exercise of the right of withdrawal before the withdrawal period has expired.

content.subtitle: Reimbursement
content.text: If you withdraw from this contract, we shall reimburse to you all payments received from you, including the costs of delivery (with the exception of the supplementary costs resulting from your choice of a type of delivery other than the least expensive type of standard delivery offered by us), without undue delay and in any event not later than fourteen days from the day on which we are informed of your decision to withdraw from this contract.

content.subtitle: Means of reimbursement
content.text: We will carry out such reimbursement using the same means of payment as you used for the initial transaction, unless you have expressly agreed otherwise; in any event, you will not incur any fees as a result of such reimbursement.

content.subtitle: Early expiry for digital content
content.text: The right of withdrawal expires prematurely if BeAFox has begun the execution of the contract after you (1) have expressly agreed that BeAFox may begin execution of the contract before expiry of the withdrawal period, and (2) have confirmed your knowledge that by giving your consent you lose your right of withdrawal upon commencement of execution of the contract (§ 356 para. 5 BGB).

content.subtitle: Compensation for early commencement
content.text: If you have requested that the services should begin during the withdrawal period, you shall pay us a reasonable amount corresponding to the proportion of the services already provided up to the time at which you inform us of your exercise of the right of withdrawal, in comparison to the total scope of services provided for in the contract.

content.subtitle: In-app purchases via app stores
content.text: For purchases made through the Apple App Store or Google Play Store, the withdrawal conditions of the respective app store operator apply. The payment contract in these cases is concluded with the app store operator. Please contact the respective app store directly for withdrawal.

content.subtitle: Website purchases via Stripe
content.text: For subscriptions concluded through our website (payment processing via Stripe), please direct your withdrawal directly to BeAFox using the contact details above. The reimbursement will be processed via Stripe to the original payment method.

content.subtitle: Note
content.text: If you wish to withdraw from the contract, please complete this form and send it back. The form is designed in accordance with Annex 2 to Article 246a § 1 para. 2 sentence 1 no. 1 and § 2 para. 2 no. 2 EGBGB.

meta.description: Accessibility statement of BeAFox UG (haftungsbeschränkt) in accordance with the German Accessibility Strengthening Act (BFSG) and EU Directive 2019/882 (European Accessibility Act).

hero.title: Accessibility Statement
hero.subtitle: Information on the digital accessibility of our website and app in accordance with the German Accessibility Strengthening Act (BFSG) and EU Directive 2019/882.

content.subtitle: Our Commitment
content.text: BeAFox UG (haftungsbeschränkt) is committed to making its website (beafox.app) and the BeAFox app accessible in accordance with the German Accessibility Strengthening Act (BFSG) and EU Directive 2019/882 (European Accessibility Act, EAA). We work continuously to improve the user experience for everyone – regardless of their abilities or the technologies they use.

content.subtitle: Scope
content.text: This statement applies to the website https://beafox.app as well as the BeAFox app for iOS and Android.

content.subtitle: Conformance Status
content.text: This website and app are partially conformant with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA and the requirements of the harmonized European standard EN 301 549. We are actively working to achieve full conformance.

content.subtitle: Reference Standards
content.text: We align our efforts with the following standards and norms:
list: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA
German Accessibility Strengthening Act (BFSG) – Implementation of EU Directive 2019/882

content.subtitle: Implemented Measures
content.text: We have already implemented the following accessibility measures:
list: Semantic HTML5 with correct heading hierarchy (h1–h6)
ARIA labels and roles for interactive elements and navigation
Keyboard navigation for all essential website functions
Responsive design for various screen sizes and devices
Text-based alternative descriptions (alt attributes) for informative images
Sufficient color contrast in accordance with WCAG 2.1 AA (contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 for normal text)
Self-hosting of fonts (Google Fonts via next/font) – no external dependencies
Structured form fields with labels and error messages

content.subtitle: Currently Known Limitations
content.text: Despite our efforts, we are aware of the following limitations, which we are actively working to resolve:
list: Some Lottie animations (JSON-based) may not yet have complete text descriptions
Certain interactive calculator components (financial calculators) may not be fully keyboard operable
Some PDF documents may not yet be fully accessible
Third-party content (e.g., embedded videos, Stripe checkout) is subject to the accessibility standards of the respective providers
The mobile app is being progressively optimized for VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android)

content.subtitle: Remediation Timeline
content.text: We have established a timeline for remedying the known limitations. Our goal is to achieve full WCAG 2.1 Level AA conformance by the end of 2026. Priority is given to the core learning platform features.

content.subtitle: Technologies Used
content.text: The accessibility of this website is based on the following technologies:
list: Next.js 14 (React 18) with server and client components

content.subtitle: Supported Browsers and Assistive Technologies
content.text: We regularly test with the following combinations:

content.subtitle: Feedback Mechanism (BFSG § 3a)
content.text: If you encounter barriers or have suggestions for improvement, please contact us. We take all feedback seriously and make every effort to address reported issues promptly.

content.subtitle: Contact Options
content.text: You can reach us through the following channels:
list: Mail: BeAFox UG (haftungsbeschränkt), Siemensweg 2, 93073 Neutraubling, Germany

content.subtitle: Response Time
content.text: We make every effort to review your feedback within 14 days and provide you with a response. For more complex inquiries, we will inform you about the expected timeline.

content.subtitle: Dispute Resolution
content.text: If you do not receive a satisfactory solution after providing feedback to us, you can contact the competent market surveillance authority. In Bavaria, this is:

content.subtitle: Competent Authority
content.text: Bayerisches Staatsministerium für Digitales

content.subtitle: Legal Basis
content.text: The enforcement procedure is governed by the provisions of the BFSG and EU Directive 2019/882. The market surveillance authority may take measures in case of violations of accessibility requirements.

content.subtitle: Testing Methodology
content.text: The assessment of accessibility is based on a self-assessment conducted by BeAFox UG. We use automated tools (Lighthouse, axe DevTools) as well as manual testing with assistive technologies.

content.subtitle: Next Planned Review
content.text: The next comprehensive accessibility review is scheduled for the third quarter of 2026. In the meantime, continuous improvements are being made.
